――あなたねえ！ なんなの、この計画は！<br>装備も物資も、ほとんど本人任せじ:
—You! What is this plan? You're leaving the equipment and supplies
almost entirely to them!
次の作戦は少数精鋭部隊による調査だ。<br>大掛かりな準備よりも本人たちの希望が:
The next operation is a recon mission by the Elite Squad. Their own
wishes take priority over large-scale preparations.
またそうやって、危険なことを……！<br>だいたい少数精鋭による作戦なんてやるべ:
There you go again with your dangerous ideas! In the first place, you
shouldn't be conducting operations with just the Elite Squad!
勝敗を決めるのは兵の数よ！<br>潤沢な兵、充分な物資による安定した軍事作戦、そ:
Victory is determined by numbers! A stable military operation with
ample troops and sufficient supplies—that's the standard approach!
大穴なんていう常識の通じない場所で、教科書通りの作戦に意味があるのか？<br>司:
Does a textbook operation even mean anything in a place like the
Abyss where common sense doesn't apply? A Commander's job isn't to increase needless casualties!
そうして２人の熱い作戦会議は、半日以上も続き――:
And so, their heated strategy session continued for more than half a
day—
――じゃあ結論として。精鋭部隊を主力に、<br>後方へサポートの部隊を派遣する…:
—So, to conclude: we make the Elite Squad the main force and
dispatch a support unit to the rear... That's acceptable, right?
分かったよ、それでいい。<br>ったく、動かす兵士が増えると準備が厄介だってのに:
Alright, that works. Jeez, more troops to move means more hassle
with preparations...
でも納得したってことは、<br>私の意見に理があると認めたってことでしょう？: But agreeing to it means you admit my argument has merit, doesn't it?
まーな。いくら精鋭部隊でも大穴の調査には不安があるだろう。<br>後詰がいるかい:
Yeah, well. Even the Elite Squad would be uneasy exploring the Abyss.
Having a rear guard makes a difference for morale.
こりゃ計画の練り直しだな。<br>しばらくは徹夜か……: Looks like we're reworking the plan. All-nighter, huh...
……悪かったわね。<br>でも、事前の準備で手を抜くと、いざって時に痛い目みるん:
...Sorry. But if you cut corners in preparation, you'll pay for it when
it counts...
さすがは騎士学校の優等生。<br>真面目だな、本当に。:
As expected of the top student at the Knight Academy. You're really
diligent.
……真面目、ね。学校では嬉しかったけれど、<br>最前線では褒め言葉じゃないって:
...'Diligent,' huh. It made me happy at school, but I'm well aware that
on the front lines, it's not exactly a compliment.
気にしすぎだ。<br>俺はソフィアの真面目なところに助けられてるよ。: You're overthinking it. Your diligence really helps me out.
少し落ち込んだソフィアの頭を撫でながら慰める。<br>すると彼女は頬を赤らめなが:
He patted the slightly dejected Sophia on the head to comfort her.
Then, her cheeks flushing, she whispered softly.
……いつもだらしなくて、作戦も自由奔放行き当たりばったり―― <br>本当にどう:
...I said you're always a mess, your strategies are all over the
place—you're really a hopeless case!
……でも私は、そんな司令官に助けられているわ。: ...And yet, it's you, Commander, who helps me.
私に足りない部分を、あなたが埋めてくれる。<br>いつだって私を成長させてくれる:
You fill in what I'm missing. You always help me grow. You, who do
that...
ソフィアは顔を真っ赤に染め、<br>精一杯の勇気で、今までの想いを言葉に紡いだ。:
Sophia's face turned bright red, and with all the courage she could
muster, she wove her long-held feelings into words.
ソフィアの思いがけない一言に、部屋の時が止まる。<br>張り詰めたような静寂が２:
At Sophia's unexpected words, time stood still in the room. A tense
silence enveloped the two of them.
（なんなのよ……心臓のドキドキやばい……。<br>こ、これ、聞こえちゃってるのか: (What is this...? My heart's pounding so hard... C-can he hear it...?)
言葉はそれだけで十分だった。ソフィアをそっと抱き寄せる。<br>２人切りの部屋に:
Those words alone were enough. He gently pulled Sophia close. In the
room that was just for the two of them, sweet time began to flow—
